comment_text.sn7.15:1.2: Mānatthaddha, literally “stiff with pride”. So stiff, apparently, he could not bow. Also used of a brahmin at DN 4:8.17.
comment_text.sn7.15:6.3: Given the context, I think nihacca here has the same sense as it does more explicitly at Thig 5.9:3.1, “bending the knee”.

root_text.sn7.15:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena mānatthaddho nāma brāhmaṇo sāvatthiyaṁ paṭivasati.
root_text.sn7.15:1.3: So neva mātaraṁ abhivādeti, na pitaraṁ abhivādeti, na ācariyaṁ abhivādeti, na jeṭṭhabhātaraṁ abhivādeti.
root_text.sn7.15:1.4: Tena kho pana samayena bhagavā mahatiyā parisāya parivuto dhammaṁ deseti.
root_text.sn7.15:1.6: “ayaṁ kho samaṇo gotamo mahatiyā parisāya parivuto dhammaṁ deseti.
root_text.sn7.15:1.8: Sace maṁ samaṇo gotamo ālapissati, ahampi taṁ ālapissāmi.
root_text.sn7.15:1.9: No ce maṁ samaṇo gotamo ālapissati, ahampi nālapissāmī”ti.
root_text.sn7.15:1.10: Atha kho mānatthaddho brāhmaṇo yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā tuṇhībhūto ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi.
root_text.sn7.15:1.13: “nāyaṁ samaṇo gotamo kiñci jānātī”ti tatova puna nivattitukāmo ahosi.
root_text.sn7.15:1.14: Atha kho bhagavā mānatthaddhassa brāhmaṇassa cetasā cetoparivitakkamaññāya mānatthaddhaṁ brāhmaṇaṁ gāthāya ajjhabhāsi:
root_text.sn7.15:3.2: “cittaṁ me samaṇo gotamo jānātī”ti tattheva bhagavato pādesu sirasā nipatitvā bhagavato pādāni mukhena ca paricumbati pāṇīhi ca parisambāhati, nāmañca sāveti:
root_text.sn7.15:3.4: Atha kho sā parisā abbhutavittajātā ahosi:
root_text.sn7.15:3.6: Ayañhi mānatthaddho brāhmaṇo neva mātaraṁ abhivādeti, na pitaraṁ abhivādeti, na ācariyaṁ abhivādeti, na jeṭṭhabhātaraṁ abhivādeti;
root_text.sn7.15:3.7: atha ca pana samaṇe gotame evarūpaṁ paramanipaccakāraṁ karotī”ti.
root_text.sn7.15:3.8: Atha kho bhagavā mānatthaddhaṁ brāhmaṇaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn7.15:3.11: Atha kho mānatthaddho brāhmaṇo sake āsane nisīditvā bhagavantaṁ gāthāya ajjhabhāsi:
root_text.sn7.15:7.1: Evaṁ vutte, mānatthaddho brāhmaṇo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn7.15:7.3: upāsakaṁ maṁ bhavaṁ gotamo dhāretu ajjatagge pāṇupetaṁ saraṇaṁ gatan”ti.

translation_text.sn7.15:1.2: Now at that time a brahmin named Stuck-Up was residing in Sāvatthī.
translation_text.sn7.15:1.3: He didn’t bow to his mother or father, his tutor, or his oldest brother.
translation_text.sn7.15:1.4: Now, at that time the Buddha was teaching Dhamma, surrounded by a large assembly.
translation_text.sn7.15:1.6: “The ascetic Gotama is teaching Dhamma, surrounded by a large assembly.
translation_text.sn7.15:1.8: If he speaks to me, I’ll speak to him.
translation_text.sn7.15:1.9: But if he doesn’t speak, neither will I.”
translation_text.sn7.15:1.10: Then the brahmin Stuck-Up went up to the Buddha, and stood silently to one side.
translation_text.sn7.15:1.11: But the Buddha didn’t speak to him.
translation_text.sn7.15:1.13: “This ascetic Gotama knows nothing!” And he wanted to go back from there right away.
translation_text.sn7.15:1.14: Then the Buddha, knowing Stuck-Up’s train of thought, addressed him in verse:
translation_text.sn7.15:2.2: if you want what’s good for you, brahmin.
translation_text.sn7.15:3.2: “The ascetic Gotama knows my mind!” He bowed with his head at the Buddha’s feet, caressing them and covering them with kisses, and pronounced his name:
translation_text.sn7.15:3.3: “Worthy Gotama, I am Stuck-Up! I am Stuck-Up!”
translation_text.sn7.15:3.6: This brahmin Stuck-Up doesn’t bow to his mother or father, his tutor, or his oldest brother.
translation_text.sn7.15:3.7: Yet he shows such utmost deference to the ascetic Gotama!”
translation_text.sn7.15:3.8: Then the Buddha said to the brahmin Stuck-Up,
translation_text.sn7.15:3.9: “Enough, brahmin. Get up, and take your own seat.
translation_text.sn7.15:3.10: For your mind has confidence in me.”
translation_text.sn7.15:3.11: Then Stuck-Up took his seat and said to the Buddha:
translation_text.sn7.15:4.1: “Regarding whom should you not be conceited?
translation_text.sn7.15:4.4: Who is it good to venerate properly?”
translation_text.sn7.15:5.4: Regarding these you should not be conceited.
translation_text.sn7.15:5.5: They are who you should respect.
translation_text.sn7.15:5.6: They are who you should esteem.
translation_text.sn7.15:5.7: And they’re who it’s good to venerate properly.
translation_text.sn7.15:6.1: And when with bent knee, not stiff with conceit,
translation_text.sn7.15:6.4: their task complete, free of defilements.”
translation_text.sn7.15:7.1: When he had spoken, the brahmin Stuck-Up said to the Buddha,
translation_text.sn7.15:7.3: From this day forth, may the worthy Gotama remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge for life.”

variant_text.sn7.15:3.4: abbhutavittajātā → abbhutacittajātā (bj, km, pts1ed, pts2ed); abbhūtacittajātā (sya-all); acchariyabbhutacittajātā (mr)
